text: I'll start by reading the key skills and locating the artifacts.

Base directory for this skill: /home/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.claude/skills/project-git

Read this before running `git diff`/`git log` on a project.

## The model

- **Workspace repo is out-of-tree too** — its git dir is
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git` with the workspace **root** as its work tree
  (driven via `--git-dir`/`--work-tree`). **`<root>/.git`** is deliberately not touched, 
  because it is reserved for a user repo. It commits `config.yaml`, scoped project files, and `.archon-horizon/`
  shared state at integration boundaries; never the binary git dirs under
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/`, locks, or other volatile internals.
- **Project repos live out-of-tree.** Every project should have an out-of-tree
  git directory at `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git`, with the project
  directory as its work tree. Project directories do not contain nested `.git/`.
- Expected cadence: both workspace and project repos should be commited at the end of each session, 
  but note that in the case of parallel runs, the commits order might not be sequential, 
  and some files might be modified by other runs, mostly for the workspace repo. However, 
  this should not be a problem in general. 

So a plain `git diff` at the root sees only a user repo (if any), never Archon's.
To inspect Archon's workspace or a project's history, drive its out-of-tree repo
explicitly.

```bash
# Workspace (manifest) history:
git --git-dir=.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git --work-tree=. log --oneline -n 20
```

## Reading a project's history / diff

Run from the workspace root:

```bash
GD=.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git
WT=<path-to-project>      # the project's `path` from the project list

git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT log --oneline -n 20
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT show <sha>
```

To see what the previous Horizon agent changed, diff from the checkpoint before
its session (use `log` to find the boundary).

## Caveats

- Every configured project is expected to have VCS enabled. If
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git` is missing, report it as a workspace setup
  issue rather than treating it as normal, but work with it, it might be a new project, or a project that was cloned from the workspace's repo (hence without the vcs committed).
- You read history; you do not commit manually. Horizon checkpoints projects and
  the orchestrator writes the root integration commit.
- The bundled author identity is `Archon Horizon <archon-horizon@local>`.

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.EntryIdeal
8	import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme
9	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PullbackQuasicoherent
10	
11	/-!
12	# Rank strata of a coherent module: geometric substrate (Nitsure §4, `n = 0`)
13	
14	This file builds the geometric layer between the entry-ideal commutative
15	algebra of `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.EntryIdeal` and the universal property
16	of the flat-locus stratification
17	(`AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification_universal` in
18	`AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric`), following the special
19	case `n = 0` of the flattening-stratification theorem in [Nitsure, §4].
20	
21	## Contents
22	
23	* §1 — presentation transport (`Module.MatrixPresentation.congr`) and
24	  fiber-rank bricks: monotonicity under `e`-generator presentations
25	  (`fiberRank_le`), invariance under linear equivalences
26	  (`Ideal.fiberRank_congr`), base change
27	  (`Ideal.fiberRank_baseChange`: the fiber of `A ⊗[R] M` at a prime `q`
28	  has the same dimension as the fiber of `M` at `q ∩ R`), and the
29	  localized-module corollary (`Ideal.fiberRank_of_isLocalizedModule`).
30	* §2 — the point ↔ prime dictionary for affine opens:
31	  membership in `Scheme.zeroLocus`/`Scheme.basicOpen` in terms of
32	  `IsAffineOpen.primeIdealOf`.
33	* §3 — presentation charts: affine opens `V` on which the section module
34	  `Γ(G, V)` admits an `e`-generator matrix presentation; stability under
35	  basic-open localization (via the quasi-coherent section-localization
36	  engine `Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` of
37	  `AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.QuotScheme`); the point-rank function and its
38	  chart-independence.
39	
40	Blueprint chapter: `Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`,
41	§`sec:flatstrat_universal`.
42	Source: [Nitsure], §4, proof of the flattening-stratification theorem,
43	special case `n = 0` (`references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/`
44	`nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex`, L1849–L1885).
45	-/
46	
47	universe u
48	
49	open TensorProduct
50	
51	/-! ## §1 Presentation transport and fiber-rank bricks -/
52	
53	namespace Module.MatrixPresentation
54	
55	section Congr
56	
57	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
58	variable {N : Type u} [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] {e m : ℕ}
59	
60	/-- Transport of a matrix presentation along a linear equivalence of the
61	presented module; the relation matrix — hence the entry ideal — is
62	unchanged. -/
63	noncomputable def congr (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) (σ : M ≃ₗ[R] N) :
64	    MatrixPresentation R N e m where
65	  relMatrix := P.relMatrix
66	  proj := σ.toLinearMap ∘ₗ P.proj
67	  surjective_proj := σ.surjective.comp P.surjective_proj
68	  exact_mulVecLin_proj := fun y => by
69	    simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_coe,
70	      LinearEquiv.map_eq_zero_iff]
71	    exact P.exact_mulVecLin_proj y
72	
73	@[simp] lemma congr_relMatrix (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
74	    (σ : M ≃ₗ[R] N) : (P.congr σ).relMatrix = P.relMatrix := rfl
75	
76	@[simp] lemma congr_entryIdeal (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m)
77	    (σ : M ≃ₗ[R] N) : (P.congr σ).entryIdeal = P.entryIdeal := rfl
78	
79	/-- An `e`-generator presentation bounds the fiber rank at every prime by
80	`e`: the base-changed projection is a surjection `κ(p)^e ↠ κ(p) ⊗ M`. -/
81	theorem fiberRank_le (P : MatrixPresentation R M e m) (p : Ideal R)
82	    [p.IsPrime] : p.fiberRank M ≤ e := by
83	  have hsurj := (P.baseChange p.ResidueField).surjective_proj
84	  have h := LinearMap.finrank_le_finrank_of_surjective
85	    (f := (P.baseChange p.ResidueField).proj) hsurj
86	  simpa [Ideal.fiberRank] using h
87	
88	end Congr
89	
90	end Module.MatrixPresentation
91	
92	namespace Ideal
93	
94	section FiberRankCongr
95	
96	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
97	variable {N : Type u} [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
98	
99	/-- The fiber rank is invariant under linear equivalences. -/
100	lemma fiberRank_congr (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime] (σ : M ≃ₗ[R] N) :
101	    p.fiberRank M = p.fiberRank N :=
102	  LinearEquiv.finrank_eq (LinearEquiv.baseChange R p.ResidueField M N σ)
103	
104	/-- Congruence of the fiber rank in the prime (avoids the dependent-motive
105	failure of `rw` through the `IsPrime` instance). -/
106	lemma fiberRank_congr_ideal {p q : Ideal R} [p.IsPrime] [q.IsPrime]
107	    (h : p = q) : p.fiberRank M = q.fiberRank M := by
108	  subst h; rfl
109	
110	end FiberRankCongr
111	
112	section FiberRankBaseChange
113	
114	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {A : Type u} [CommRing A] [Algebra R A]
115	variable (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
116	
117	/-- **Base-change invariance of the fiber rank**: for an `R`-algebra `A`
118	and a prime `q` of `A` lying over `p = q ∩ R`, the fiber of `A ⊗[R] M` at
119	`q` is the base change of the fiber of `M` at `p` along the residue-field
120	extension `κ(p) → κ(q)`; in particular the fiber dimensions agree.  No
121	flatness or finiteness hypotheses. -/
122	theorem fiberRank_baseChange (q : Ideal A) [q.IsPrime] :
123	    q.fiberRank (A ⊗[R] M) = (q.comap (algebraMap R A)).fiberRank M := by
124	  set p := q.comap (algebraMap R A) with hp
125	  -- the residue-field extension `κ(p) → κ(q)`; the `R`- and `A`-algebra
126	  -- structures on the residue fields are the canonical ones
127	  letI : Algebra p.ResidueField q.ResidueField :=
128	    (Ideal.ResidueField.map p q (algebraMap R A) rfl).toAlgebra
129	  haveI : IsScalarTower R p.ResidueField q.ResidueField := by
130	    refine .of_algebraMap_eq fun r => ?_
131	    show algebraMap R q.ResidueField r =
132	      Ideal.ResidueField.map p q (algebraMap R A) rfl (algebraMap R p.ResidueField r)
133	    rw [Ideal.ResidueField.map_algebraMap,
134	      IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply R A q.ResidueField]
135	  -- cancel the two base changes
136	  have e1 : q.Fiber (A ⊗[R] M) ≃ₗ[q.ResidueField]
137	      q.ResidueField ⊗[R] M :=
138	    AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange R A q.ResidueField q.ResidueField M
139	  have e2 : q.ResidueField ⊗[R] M ≃ₗ[q.ResidueField]
140	      q.ResidueField ⊗[p.ResidueField] (p.Fiber M) :=
141	    (AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange R p.ResidueField q.ResidueField
142	      q.ResidueField M).symm
143	  rw [Ideal.fiberRank, LinearEquiv.finrank_eq (e1.trans e2),
144	    Module.finrank_baseChange, Ideal.fiberRank]
145	
146	end FiberRankBaseChange
147	
148	section FiberRankLocalized
149	
150	variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
151	variable {R' : Type u} [CommRing R'] [Algebra R R'] (S : Submonoid R)
152	variable [IsLocalization S R']
153	variable {N : Type u} [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module R' N]
154	variable [IsScalarTower R R' N]
155	
156	/-- Localized modules have the same fiber ranks: for a localization
157	`R' = S⁻¹R`, a localized module `N = S⁻¹M` and a prime `q` of `R'`, the
158	fiber rank of `N` at `q` equals that of `M` at the contraction `q ∩ R`. -/
159	theorem fiberRank_of_isLocalizedModule (l : M →ₗ[R] N)
160	    [IsLocalizedModule S l] (q : Ideal R') [q.IsPrime] :
161	    q.fiberRank N = (q.comap (algebraMap R R')).fiberRank M := by
162	  have hbc := IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange S R' l
163	  exact (Ideal.fiberRank_congr q hbc.equiv.symm).trans
164	    (Ideal.fiberRank_baseChange M q)
165	
166	end FiberRankLocalized
167	
168	end Ideal
169	
170	/-! ## §2 The point ↔ prime dictionary on affine opens -/
171	
172	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen
173	
174	variable {X : Scheme.{u}} {U : X.Opens} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
175	
176	include hU in
177	/-- A point of an affine open lies in the zero locus of an ideal of
178	sections iff its prime contains the ideal. -/
179	lemma mem_zeroLocus_iff_le_primeIdealOf (I : Ideal Γ(X, U)) (x : U) :
180	    (x : X) ∈ X.zeroLocus (U := U) (I : Set Γ(X, U)) ↔
181	      I ≤ (hU.primeIdealOf x).asIdeal := by
182	  constructor
183	  · intro hx
184	    have h4 : (x : X) ∈ hU.fromSpec '' PrimeSpectrum.zeroLocus (I : Set Γ(X, U)) := by
185	      rw [hU.fromSpec_image_zeroLocus]
186	      exact ⟨hx, x.2⟩
187	    obtain ⟨y, hy, hyx⟩ := h4
188	    have hinj : Function.Injective hU.fromSpec :=
189	      hU.fromSpec.isOpenEmbedding.injective
190	    have hyeq : y = hU.primeIdealOf x :=
191	      hinj (by rw [hyx, hU.fromSpec_primeIdealOf x])
192	    subst hyeq
193	    exact fun s hs => (PrimeSpectrum.mem_zeroLocus _ _).mp hy hs
194	  · intro hle
195	    have h1 : hU.primeIdealOf x ∈ PrimeSpectrum.zeroLocus (I : Set Γ(X, U)) :=
196	      (PrimeSpectrum.mem_zeroLocus _ _).mpr fun s hs => hle hs
197	    have h2 : (x : X) ∈ hU.fromSpec '' PrimeSpectrum.zeroLocus (I : Set Γ(X, U)) :=
198	      ⟨_, h1, hU.fromSpec_primeIdealOf x⟩
199	    rw [hU.fromSpec_image_zeroLocus] at h2
200	    exact h2.1
201	
202	include hU in
203	/-- A point of an affine open lies in a basic open iff the section avoids
204	its prime. -/
205	lemma mem_basicOpen_iff_notMem_primeIdealOf (f : Γ(X, U)) (x : U) :
206	    (x : X) ∈ X.basicOpen f ↔ f ∉ (hU.primeIdealOf x).asIdeal := by
207	  have hpre : hU.primeIdealOf x ∈ hU.fromSpec ⁻¹ᵁ X.basicOpen f ↔
208	      f ∉ (hU.primeIdealOf x).asIdeal := by
209	    rw [hU.fromSpec_preimage_basicOpen]
210	    exact PrimeSpectrum.mem_basicOpen _ _
211	  refine Iff.trans ?_ hpre
212	  show _ ↔ hU.fromSpec (hU.primeIdealOf x) ∈ X.basicOpen f
213	  rw [hU.fromSpec_primeIdealOf x]
214	
215	end AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen
216	
217	/-! ## §3 Presentation charts and the point rank -/
218	
219	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
220	
221	open Module CategoryTheory
222	
223	/-- The `appLE` of the identity morphism is the plain restriction map. -/
224	lemma Scheme.id_appLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {U V : X.Opens}
225	    (e : V ≤ (𝟙 X : X ⟶ X) ⁻¹ᵁ U) :
226	    (𝟙 X : X ⟶ X).appLE U V e =
227	      X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (show V ≤ U from e)).op := by
228	  simp only [Scheme.Hom.appLE, Scheme.Hom.id_app]
229	  exact Category.id_comp _
230	
231	namespace Scheme.Modules
232	
233	variable {X : Scheme.{u}} (G : X.Modules)
234	
235	/-- The canonical `Γ(X, U)`-module structure on the sections of `G` over a
236	basic open of `U` (restriction of scalars along the restriction map);
237	matches the canonical algebra instance
238	`Scheme.algebra_section_section_basicOpen`. -/
239	noncomputable local instance moduleSectionBasicOpen
240	    {U : X.Opens} (f : Γ(X, U)) :
241	    Module Γ(X, U) Γ(G, X.basicOpen f) :=
242	  Module.compHom _ (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op).hom
243	
244	local instance isScalarTowerSectionBasicOpen {U : X.Opens} (f : Γ(X, U)) :
245	    IsScalarTower Γ(X, U) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) Γ(G, X.basicOpen f) :=
246	  .of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
247	
248	/-- An affine open `V` is an **`e`-presentation chart** for the module `G`
249	if the section module `Γ(G, V)` admits a matrix presentation with `e`
250	generators [Nitsure §4, `n = 0`: the local right-exact presentation
251	`𝒪_V^{⊕m} →ψ 𝒪_V^{⊕e} → F|_V → 0`]. -/
252	def IsPresentationChart (e : ℕ) (V : X.affineOpens) : Prop :=
253	  ∃ mm : ℕ, Nonempty (MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mm)
254	
255	variable [G.IsQuasicoherent]
256	
257	/-- The section module of a quasi-coherent module over a basic open, as a
258	matrix presentation: an `e`-generator presentation over an affine open
259	localizes to an `e`-generator presentation over each basic open, with the
260	restricted relation matrix. -/
261	noncomputable def MatrixPresentationBasicOpen {e mm : ℕ} {V : X.affineOpens}
262	    (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mm) (f : Γ(X, V.1)) :
263	    MatrixPresentation Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) Γ(G, X.basicOpen f) e mm :=
264	  haveI := V.2.isLocalization_basicOpen f
265	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen G V.2 f
266	  (P.baseChange Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)).congr
267	    (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers f) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)
268	      (restrictBasicOpenₗ G f)).equiv
269	
270	@[simp] lemma matrixPresentationBasicOpen_relMatrix {e mm : ℕ}
271	    {V : X.affineOpens} (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mm)
272	    (f : Γ(X, V.1)) :
273	    (MatrixPresentationBasicOpen G P f).relMatrix =
274	      P.relMatrix.map (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op).hom :=
275	  rfl
276	
277	/-- Presentation charts are stable under passing to basic opens. -/
278	theorem IsPresentationChart.basicOpen {e : ℕ} {V : X.affineOpens}
279	    (h : IsPresentationChart G e V) (f : Γ(X, V.1)) :
280	    IsPresentationChart G e (X.affineBasicOpen f) := by
281	  obtain ⟨mm, ⟨P⟩⟩ := h
282	  exact ⟨mm, ⟨MatrixPresentationBasicOpen G P f⟩⟩
283	
284	/-- The dimension of the fiber of `G` at a point `x` of an affine open
285	chart `V`: the fiber rank of the section module `Γ(G, V)` at the prime of
286	`x` [Nitsure §4: `e = dim_{κ(s)} F(s)`]. -/
287	noncomputable def chartFiberRank {V : X.affineOpens} (x : X)
288	    (hx : x ∈ V.1) : ℕ :=
289	  ((V.2.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal).fiberRank Γ(G, V.1)
290	
291	omit [SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent G] in
292	lemma chartFiberRank_congr_chart {V W : X.affineOpens} (h : V = W) (x : X)
293	    (hxV : x ∈ V.1) (hxW : x ∈ W.1) :
294	    chartFiberRank G x hxV = chartFiberRank G x hxW := by
295	  subst h; rfl
296	
297	/-- The chart fiber rank is unchanged by passing to a basic open of the
298	chart. -/
299	theorem chartFiberRank_basicOpen {V : X.affineOpens} (f : Γ(X, V.1))
300	    (x : X) (hx : x ∈ X.basicOpen f) :
301	    chartFiberRank G (V := V) x (X.basicOpen_le f hx) =
302	      chartFiberRank G (V := X.affineBasicOpen f) x hx := by
303	  haveI := V.2.isLocalization_basicOpen f
304	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen G V.2 f
305	  have hloc := Ideal.fiberRank_of_isLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f)
306	    (restrictBasicOpenₗ G f)
307	    (((V.2.basicOpen f).primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal)
308	  have hle : X.basicOpen f ≤ (𝟙 X : X ⟶ X) ⁻¹ᵁ V.1 := X.basicOpen_le f
309	  have h1 := IsAffineOpen.comap_primeIdealOf_appLE (f := (𝟙 X : X ⟶ X))
310	    V.1 V.2 (X.basicOpen f) (V.2.basicOpen f) hle hx
311	  rw [Scheme.id_appLE] at h1
312	  have hpt : V.2.primeIdealOf ⟨(𝟙 X : X ⟶ X) x, hle hx⟩ =
313	      V.2.primeIdealOf ⟨x, X.basicOpen_le f hx⟩ := by
314	    congr 1
315	  have hcomap :
316	      (((V.2.basicOpen f).primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal).comap
317	          (algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)) =
318	        (V.2.primeIdealOf ⟨x, X.basicOpen_le f hx⟩).asIdeal := by
319	    have h2 : algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) =
320	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op).hom := rfl
321	    rw [h2, ← hpt]
322	    exact congrArg PrimeSpectrum.asIdeal h1
323	  calc chartFiberRank G (V := V) x (X.basicOpen_le f hx)
324	      = ((((V.2.basicOpen f).primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal).comap
325	          (algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f))).fiberRank Γ(G, V.1) :=
326	        Ideal.fiberRank_congr_ideal hcomap.symm
327	    _ = (((V.2.basicOpen f).primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal).fiberRank
328	          Γ(G, X.basicOpen f) := hloc.symm
329	    _ = chartFiberRank G (V := X.affineBasicOpen f) x hx := rfl
330	
331	/-- **Chart-independence of the fiber rank**: two affine charts through the
332	same point give the same fiber rank.  Proof: refine to a common basic open
333	(`exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter`) and localize both sides. -/
334	theorem chartFiberRank_eq {V W : X.affineOpens} (x : X)
335	    (hxV : x ∈ V.1) (hxW : x ∈ W.1) :
336	    chartFiberRank G x hxV = chartFiberRank G x hxW := by
337	  obtain ⟨f, g, hfg, hxf⟩ :=
338	    exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter V.2 W.2 x ⟨hxV, hxW⟩
339	  have hxg : x ∈ X.basicOpen g := by rw [← hfg]; exact hxf
340	  have h1 := chartFiberRank_basicOpen G (V := V) f x hxf
341	  have h2 := chartFiberRank_basicOpen G (V := W) g x hxg
342	  have h3 : chartFiberRank G (V := X.affineBasicOpen f) x hxf =
343	      chartFiberRank G (V := X.affineBasicOpen g) x hxg :=
344	    chartFiberRank_congr_chart G (Subtype.ext hfg) x hxf hxg
345	  calc chartFiberRank G x hxV
346	      = chartFiberRank G (V := V) x (X.basicOpen_le f hxf) := rfl
347	    _ = chartFiberRank G (V := X.affineBasicOpen f) x hxf := h1
348	    _ = chartFiberRank G (V := X.affineBasicOpen g) x hxg := h3
349	    _ = chartFiberRank G (V := W) x (X.basicOpen_le g hxg) := h2.symm
350	    _ = chartFiberRank G x hxW := rfl

352	variable (X) in
353	/-- The **point rank** of a quasi-coherent module: the dimension of its
354	fiber at the point, computed through any affine chart
355	(`pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank`). -/
356	noncomputable def pointRank (x : X) : ℕ :=
357	  chartFiberRank G x (V := ⟨(exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset
358	      (x := x) (U := ⊤) trivial).choose,
359	      (exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (x := x) (U := ⊤)
360	        trivial).choose_spec.1⟩)
361	    (exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (x := x) (U := ⊤)
362	      trivial).choose_spec.2.1
363	
364	theorem pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank {V : X.affineOpens} (x : X)
365	    (hx : x ∈ V.1) : pointRank X G x = chartFiberRank G x hx :=
366	  chartFiberRank_eq G x _ hx

368	/-! ## §4 Locality and chart-independence of entry-ideal membership -/
369	
370	omit [SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent G] in
371	/-- Restriction of sections is transitive (any parallel pair of `Opens`
372	homs agree, so the composite collapses). -/
373	lemma presheaf_res_res {A B C : X.Opens} (j : B ⟶ A) (i : C ⟶ B)
374	    (k : C ⟶ A) (x : Γ(X, A)) :
375	    X.presheaf.map i.op (X.presheaf.map j.op x) = X.presheaf.map k.op x := by
376	  rw [← CommRingCat.comp_apply, ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp,
377	    Subsingleton.elim (i ≫ j) k]
378	
379	/-- Transport of a matrix presentation of the sections of `G` along an
380	equality of opens. -/
381	def transportOpens {U V : X.Opens} (h : U = V) {e mm : ℕ}
382	    (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, U) Γ(G, U) e mm) :
383	    MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V) Γ(G, V) e mm := h ▸ P
384	
385	omit [SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent G] in
386	/-- The entry ideal of a transported presentation is the image of the
387	entry ideal under the `eqToHom` restriction. -/
388	lemma entryIdeal_transportOpens {U V : X.Opens} (h : U = V) {e mm : ℕ}
389	    (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, U) Γ(G, U) e mm) :
390	    (transportOpens G h P).entryIdeal =
391	      P.entryIdeal.map (X.presheaf.map (eqToHom h.symm).op).hom := by
392	  subst h
393	  simp only [transportOpens, eqToHom_refl, op_id, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
394	  exact (Ideal.map_id _).symm
395	
396	variable {e : ℕ}
397	
398	/-- The entry ideal of the basic-open restriction of a presentation is the
399	image ideal of the entry ideal. -/
400	lemma entryIdeal_matrixPresentationBasicOpen {mm : ℕ} {V : X.affineOpens}
401	    (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mm) (f : Γ(X, V.1)) :
402	    (MatrixPresentationBasicOpen G P f).entryIdeal =
403	      P.entryIdeal.map (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op).hom := by
404	  haveI := V.2.isLocalization_basicOpen f
405	  haveI := Scheme.Modules.isLocalizedModule_basicOpen G V.2 f
406	  rw [MatrixPresentationBasicOpen, Module.MatrixPresentation.congr_entryIdeal,
407	    Module.MatrixPresentation.entryIdeal_baseChange]
408	  rfl
409	
410	/-- **Locality of entry-ideal membership on a chart**: if every point of
411	the chart has a basic open on which the restriction of `r` lands in the
412	entry ideal of the restricted presentation, then `r` is in the entry
413	ideal. -/
414	theorem mem_entryIdeal_of_locally {mm : ℕ} {V : X.affineOpens}
415	    (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mm) (r : Γ(X, V.1))
416	    (H : ∀ x ∈ V.1, ∃ g : Γ(X, V.1), x ∈ X.basicOpen g ∧
417	      X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le g)).op r ∈
418	        (MatrixPresentationBasicOpen G P g).entryIdeal) :
419	    r ∈ P.entryIdeal := by
420	  classical
421	  choose g hxg hgmem using H
422	  -- the chosen basic opens cover the chart, so the `g x` span the unit
423	  -- ideal
424	  have hspan : Ideal.span (Set.range fun x : V.1 => g x.1 x.2) = ⊤ := by
425	    rw [← V.2.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff]
426	    refine le_antisymm (iSup_le fun r => X.basicOpen_le _) fun y hy => ?_
427	    refine TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr
428	      ⟨⟨_, ⟨⟨y, hy⟩, rfl⟩⟩, hxg y hy⟩
429	  refine Submodule.mem_of_span_eq_top_of_smul_pow_mem P.entryIdeal _ hspan r
430	    (fun ⟨_, x, rfl⟩ => ?_)
431	  -- membership after localization at `g x` gives a power `g x ^ n • r ∈ I`
432	  haveI := V.2.isLocalization_basicOpen (g x.1 x.2)
433	  have h1 := hgmem x.1 x.2
434	  rw [entryIdeal_matrixPresentationBasicOpen] at h1
435	  have h2 : algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen (g x.1 x.2)) r ∈
436	      P.entryIdeal.map (algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen (g x.1 x.2))) := h1
437	  obtain ⟨s, hs, hmem⟩ :=
438	    (IsLocalization.algebraMap_mem_map_algebraMap_iff
439	      (Submonoid.powers (g x.1 x.2)) _ _ _).mp h2
440	  obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := hs
441	  exact ⟨n, by simpa [smul_eq_mul] using hmem⟩
442	
443	omit [SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent G] in
444	/-- Restriction along `U ≤ U` is the identity. -/
445	lemma presheaf_map_self {U : X.Opens} (h : U ≤ U) (r : Γ(X, U)) :
446	    X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op r = r := by
447	  rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE h) (𝟙 U), op_id,
448	    CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
449	  rfl
450	
451	/-- **Chart-to-chart transfer of the entry-ideal condition**: if the
452	restriction of `r : Γ(X, U)` to a presentation chart `W ≤ U` lies in the
453	entry ideal of `P_W`, then at every point of `W ⊓ V` for a second chart
454	`V ≤ U` there is a basic open of `V` on which the restriction of `r` lies
455	in the entry ideal of the restricted `P_V`.  Presentation-independence of
456	the entry ideal (`Module.MatrixPresentation.entryIdeal_eq`) at a common
457	basic open is the engine. -/
458	theorem exists_basicOpen_mem_entryIdeal_of_chart {U : X.Opens}
459	    (r : Γ(X, U)) {V W : X.affineOpens} (hVU : V.1 ≤ U) (hWU : W.1 ≤ U)
460	    {mmV mmW : ℕ}
461	    (PV : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mmV)
462	    (PW : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, W.1) Γ(G, W.1) e mmW)
463	    (hW : X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hWU).op r ∈ PW.entryIdeal)
464	    (x : X) (hxV : x ∈ V.1) (hxW : x ∈ W.1) :
465	    ∃ g : Γ(X, V.1), x ∈ X.basicOpen g ∧
466	      X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le g)).op
467	          (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op r) ∈
468	        (MatrixPresentationBasicOpen G PV g).entryIdeal := by
469	  obtain ⟨f, g, hfg, hxf⟩ :=
470	    exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter W.2 V.2 x ⟨hxW, hxV⟩
471	  have hbofU : X.basicOpen f ≤ U := (X.basicOpen_le f).trans hWU
472	  have hbogU : X.basicOpen g ≤ U := (X.basicOpen_le g).trans hVU
473	  refine ⟨g, hfg ▸ hxf, ?_⟩
474	  -- rewrite the doubly-restricted section as an `eqToHom`-transport of the
475	  -- `W`-side restriction
476	  have hz : X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le g)).op
477	      (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op r) =
478	      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom hfg.symm).op
479	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op
480	          (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hWU).op r)) := by
481	    rw [presheaf_res_res (homOfLE hVU)
482	      (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le g)) (homOfLE hbogU),
483	      presheaf_res_res (homOfLE hWU)
484	      (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)) (homOfLE hbofU),
485	      presheaf_res_res (homOfLE hbofU)
486	      (eqToHom hfg.symm) (homOfLE hbogU)]
487	  rw [hz,
488	    ← Module.MatrixPresentation.entryIdeal_eq
489	      (transportOpens G hfg (MatrixPresentationBasicOpen G PW f))
490	      (MatrixPresentationBasicOpen G PV g),
491	    entryIdeal_transportOpens]
492	  refine Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ ?_
493	  rw [entryIdeal_matrixPresentationBasicOpen]
494	  exact Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hW

496	/-! ## §5 The strata ideal -/
497	
498	variable (e) in
499	/-- The rank-`e` **strata ideal** at an affine open `U`: the intersection,
500	over all `e`-generator presentation charts `V ≤ U`, of the preimages of the
501	entry ideals under restriction [Nitsure §4, `n = 0`: the ideal sheaf of the
502	canonical scheme structure on the rank-`e` locus]. -/
503	noncomputable def strataIdeal (U : X.affineOpens) : Ideal Γ(X, U.1) :=
504	  ⨅ (V : X.affineOpens) (h : V.1 ≤ U.1) (mm : ℕ)
505	    (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mm),
506	    P.entryIdeal.comap (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op).hom
507	
508	omit [SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent G] in
509	lemma mem_strataIdeal_iff {U : X.affineOpens} {r : Γ(X, U.1)} :
510	    r ∈ strataIdeal G e U ↔ ∀ (V : X.affineOpens) (h : V.1 ≤ U.1) (mm : ℕ)
511	      (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mm),
512	      X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h).op r ∈ P.entryIdeal := by
513	  simp only [strataIdeal, Submodule.mem_iInf, Ideal.mem_comap]
514	
515	/-- **Evaluation of the strata ideal on a presentation chart**: on an
516	`e`-presentation chart the strata ideal is the entry ideal of any (hence
517	every) `e`-generator presentation. -/
518	theorem strataIdeal_eq_entryIdeal {V : X.affineOpens} {mm : ℕ}
519	    (P : MatrixPresentation Γ(X, V.1) Γ(G, V.1) e mm) :
520	    strataIdeal G e V = P.entryIdeal := by
521	  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
522	  · intro r hr
523	    have h := (mem_strataIdeal_iff G).mp hr V le_rfl mm P
524	    rwa [presheaf_map_self] at h
525	  · intro r hr
526	    rw [mem_strataIdeal_iff]
527	    intro W hWV mmW Q
528	    refine mem_entryIdeal_of_locally G Q _ (fun x hx => ?_)
529	    exact exists_basicOpen_mem_entryIdeal_of_chart G r hWV le_rfl Q P
530	      (by rwa [presheaf_map_self]) x hx (hWV hx)
531	
532	/-- Membership in the strata ideal follows from membership at a family of
533	charts covering `U`. -/
534	theorem mem_strataIdeal_of_charts {U : X.affineOpens} (r : Γ(X, U.1))
535	    {ι : Type*} (Vc : ι → X.affineOpens) (hVcU : ∀ i, (Vc i).1 ≤ U.1)
536	    (mmc : ι → ℕ)
537	    (Pc : ∀ i, MatrixPresentation Γ(X, (Vc i).1) Γ(G, (Vc i).1) e (mmc i))
538	    (hmem : ∀ i, X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hVcU i)).op r ∈ (Pc i).entryIdeal)
539	    (hcov : ∀ x ∈ U.1, ∃ i, x ∈ (Vc i).1) :
540	    r ∈ strataIdeal G e U := by
541	  rw [mem_strataIdeal_iff]
542	  intro W hWU mmW Q
543	  refine mem_entryIdeal_of_locally G Q _ (fun x hx => ?_)
544	  obtain ⟨i, hxi⟩ := hcov x (hWU hx)
545	  exact exists_basicOpen_mem_entryIdeal_of_chart G r hWU (hVcU i) Q (Pc i)
546	    (hmem i) x hx hxi
547	
548	variable (e) in
549	/-- The standing hypothesis of the strata construction: every point of `X`
550	lies in an `e`-presentation chart.  Holds on the union of all
551	`e`-presentation charts, the ambient space of the rank-`e` stratum. -/
552	def ChartsCover : Prop :=
553	  ∀ x : X, ∃ V : X.affineOpens, x ∈ V.1 ∧ IsPresentationChart G e V
554	
555	/-- Under `ChartsCover`, every affine open of `X` is covered by finitely
556	many `e`-presentation charts contained in it. -/
557	theorem ChartsCover.exists_finite_charts (hcov : ChartsCover G e)
558	    (U : X.affineOpens) :
559	    ∃ (n : ℕ) (Vc : Fin n → X.affineOpens) (mmc : Fin n → ℕ)
560	      (Pc : ∀ i, MatrixPresentation Γ(X, (Vc i).1) Γ(G, (Vc i).1) e (mmc i)),
561	      (∀ i, (Vc i).1 ≤ U.1) ∧ ∀ x ∈ U.1, ∃ i, x ∈ (Vc i).1 := by
562	  classical
563	  -- the subtype of presentation charts contained in `U`
564	  set J := {V : X.affineOpens // V.1 ≤ U.1 ∧ IsPresentationChart G e V}
565	  -- they cover `U`: shrink a global chart through a simultaneous basic open
566	  have hJcov : (U.1 : Set X) ⊆ ⋃ j : J, ((j.1.1 : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : Set X) := by
567	    intro x hx
568	    obtain ⟨V, hxV, hVchart⟩ := hcov x
569	    obtain ⟨f, g, hfg, hxf⟩ :=
570	      exists_basicOpen_le_affine_inter U.2 V.2 x ⟨hx, hxV⟩
571	    have hle : (X.affineBasicOpen g).1 ≤ U.1 := by
572	      show X.basicOpen g ≤ U.1
573	      rw [← hfg]; exact X.basicOpen_le f
574	    refine Set.mem_iUnion.mpr
575	      ⟨⟨X.affineBasicOpen g, hle, hVchart.basicOpen G g⟩, ?_⟩
576	    show x ∈ X.basicOpen g
577	    rw [← hfg]; exact hxf
578	  -- extract a finite subcover by quasi-compactness of the affine open
579	  obtain ⟨T, hT⟩ := U.2.isCompact.elim_finite_subcover
580	    (fun j : J => ((j.1.1 : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) : Set X))
581	    (fun j => j.1.1.2) hJcov
582	  -- enumerate and choose presentations
583	  obtain ⟨n, eT⟩ : ∃ n, Nonempty (T ≃ Fin n) := ⟨T.card, ⟨T.equivFin⟩⟩
584	  obtain ⟨eT⟩ := eT
585	  choose mmc Pc using fun j : T => (j.1.2.2 : IsPresentationChart G e j.1.1)
586	  refine ⟨n, fun i => (eT.symm i).1.1, fun i => mmc (eT.symm i),
587	    fun i => (Pc (eT.symm i)).some, fun i => (eT.symm i).1.2.1,
588	    fun x hx => ?_⟩
589	  obtain ⟨j, hjT, hxj⟩ := Set.mem_iUnion₂.mp (hT hx)
590	  exact ⟨eT ⟨j, hjT⟩, by simpa using hxj⟩
591	
592	/-- Restriction to the relative basic open computes the localization
593	fraction: restricting `mk' y (f₀ ^ n)` from `X.basicOpen f₀` to the basic
594	open of `f₀|_V` inside a smaller affine `V` gives
595	`mk' (y|_V) ((f₀|_V) ^ n)`. -/
596	theorem res_mk'_basicOpen {U : X.affineOpens} (f₀ : Γ(X, U.1))
597	    {V : X.affineOpens} (hVU : V.1 ≤ U.1) (y : Γ(X, U.1)) (n : ℕ)
598	    (h₁ : X.basicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op f₀) ≤ X.basicOpen f₀) :
599	    haveI := U.2.isLocalization_basicOpen f₀
600	    haveI := V.2.isLocalization_basicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op f₀)
601	    X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h₁).op
602	        (IsLocalization.mk' Γ(X, X.basicOpen f₀) y
603	          (⟨f₀ ^ n, pow_mem (Submonoid.mem_powers f₀) n⟩ :
604	            Submonoid.powers f₀)) =
605	      IsLocalization.mk' Γ(X, X.basicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op f₀))
606	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op y)
607	        (⟨(X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op f₀) ^ n,
608	          pow_mem (Submonoid.mem_powers _) n⟩ :
609	          Submonoid.powers (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op f₀)) := by
610	  haveI := U.2.isLocalization_basicOpen f₀
611	  haveI := V.2.isLocalization_basicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op f₀)
612	  set fv := X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op f₀ with hfv
613	  rw [eq_comm, IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq_mul]
614	  -- both sides are restrictions from `Γ(X, X.basicOpen f₀)`
615	  have hbase : (algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen fv)) =
616	      (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le fv)).op).hom := rfl
617	  have hcalc : ∀ z : Γ(X, U.1),
618	      algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen fv)
619	          (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op z) =
620	        X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h₁).op
621	          (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f₀)).op z) := by
622	    intro z
623	    rw [hbase]
624	    show X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le fv)).op
625	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hVU).op z) = _
626	    rw [presheaf_res_res (homOfLE hVU) (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le fv))
627	        (homOfLE ((X.basicOpen_le fv).trans hVU)),
628	      presheaf_res_res (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f₀)) (homOfLE h₁)
629	        (homOfLE ((X.basicOpen_le fv).trans hVU))]
630	  rw [hcalc y]
631	  have hmul : (algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen fv)) ↑(⟨fv ^ n,
632	      pow_mem (Submonoid.mem_powers _) n⟩ : Submonoid.powers fv) =
633	      X.presheaf.map (homOfLE h₁).op
634	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f₀)).op (f₀ ^ n)) := by
635	    show algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen fv) (fv ^ n) = _
636	    rw [map_pow, hcalc f₀, ← map_pow, ← map_pow]
637	  rw [hmul, ← map_mul]
638	  congr 1
639	  -- `mk' y (f₀^n) * (f₀^n)|_{D₀} = y|_{D₀}` is the defining property
640	  have hspec := IsLocalization.mk'_spec Γ(X, X.basicOpen f₀) y
641	    (⟨f₀ ^ n, pow_mem (Submonoid.mem_powers f₀) n⟩ : Submonoid.powers f₀)
642	  calc X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f₀)).op y
643	      = IsLocalization.mk' Γ(X, X.basicOpen f₀) y
644	          (⟨f₀ ^ n, pow_mem (Submonoid.mem_powers f₀) n⟩ :
645	            Submonoid.powers f₀) *
646	        algebraMap Γ(X, U.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f₀) (f₀ ^ n) := hspec.symm
647	    _ = _ := rfl
648	
649	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
650	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
651	/-- **The strata ideal localizes along basic opens** (quasi-coherence of
652	the strata ideal; the `map_ideal_basicOpen` field of the ideal-sheaf
653	datum).  [Nitsure §4, `n = 0`: the local strata glue by their universal
654	property.] -/
655	theorem map_strataIdeal_basicOpen (hcov : ChartsCover G e)
656	    (U : X.affineOpens) (f₀ : Γ(X, U.1)) :
657	    (strataIdeal G e U).map
658	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f₀)).op).hom =
659	      strataIdeal G e (X.affineBasicOpen f₀) := by
660	  haveI := U.2.isLocalization_basicOpen f₀
661	  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
662	  · -- restriction preserves the chart conditions
663	    rw [Ideal.map_le_iff_le_comap]
664	    intro r hr
665	    refine (mem_strataIdeal_iff G).mpr (fun W hWD mmW Q => ?_)
666	    have hWU : W.1 ≤ U.1 := hWD.trans (X.basicOpen_le f₀)
667	    have h := (mem_strataIdeal_iff G).mp hr W hWU mmW Q
668	    have heq := presheaf_res_res (X := X) (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f₀))
669	      (homOfLE hWD) (homOfLE hWU) r
670	    show X.presheaf.map (homOfLE hWD).op
671	      (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f₀)).op r) ∈ Q.entryIdeal
672	    exact heq.symm ▸ h
673	  · -- a section satisfying the conditions over the basic open comes from
674	    -- the strata ideal after clearing a power of `f₀`

text:
## Verdict: CONVERGING — genuine, math-sound progress

Session 0067 (T12) added one real 806-LOC file, `EntryIdealStratum.lean`, that constructs the rank-`e` stratum as a closed subscheme for the Nitsure n=0 stratification. I reviewed the three flagged soundness risks and the underlying `EntryIdeal.lean` lemmas. All check out — no vacuity, no off-by-one, no weaker-than-named statements. The report is honest and matches the diff. Filed one clean-review note: **I-0101** (info).

### Soundness findings (all sound)

**1. `fiberRank_baseChange` (L122-144) — unconditional, no flatness.** Correct. Fiber rank = `dim_{κ(p)}(κ(p) ⊗ M)`, which is base-change invariant with zero hypotheses. Proof is `AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange` twice + `Module.finrank_baseChange`. The "No flatness or finiteness hypotheses" claim in the docstring is accurate.

**2. `map_strataIdeal_basicOpen` (L655) quasi-coherence — genuinely noetherian-free.** The denominator-clearing argument gets its finiteness from affine quasi-compactness (`U.2.isCompact.elim_finite_subcover` in `ChartsCover.exists_finite_charts`), then a `Finset.sup` of per-chart clearing powers. No noetherian hypothesis anywhere. Sound.

**3. `mem_range_stratumι_iff` (L777) — support = rank EXACTLY e, not ≤ e.** No slip. It reduces to `EntryIdeal.entryIdeal_le_prime_iff : entryIdeal ≤ p ↔ p.fiberRank M = e`, whose both directions are proved genuinely (entry ideal = ideal of 1×1 minors; `⊆ p ⟺ relMatrix ≡ 0 mod p ⟺` fiber has full dimension `e`). Chart-independence (`chartFiberRank_eq`, `pointRank_eq_chartFiberRank`) is genuinely established via common-basic-open refinement + `fiberRank_of_isLocalizedModule`, not asserted.

File is sorry-free and axiom-clean (`#print axioms` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` per report; the two `grep` hits for "sorry" are inside docstring prose, not tactics).

### One scope caveat (not a bug), recorded in I-0101
`stratum` / `stratumι` / `strataData` / `map_strataIdeal_basicOpen` all take `hcov : ChartsCover G e`. Since an `e`-generator presentation caps fiber rank at `e` (`fiberRank_le`), `ChartsCover G e` forces fiber rank ≤ e at *every* point of X. So "rank-`e` stratum as a closed subscheme of X" is only constructed when `e` globally bounds the fiber rank (i.e. after restricting to `{rank ≤ e}`, or `e` maximal). This is faithful to Nitsure's inductive construction and is an honestly-named `Prop` hypothesis, not hidden — but the downstream `flatLocusStratification_universal` (Stage D/E, still 1 deliberate sorry in `GenericFlatnessGeometric.lean`) will have to discharge `ChartsCover` for the actual base.

### Throughput
Three incremental commits (`b90ff8e1` §1-3, `26ac8c7f` §4-5, `fbd96364` §6), each landing a coherent slice; build green (8584 jobs). The report correctly states the task is not complete (Stage D/E remains) and flags blueprint debt (~25 new decls unblueprinted, tracked in I-0100) and two benign linter warnings. No churn, no sorry-stall, no artificial disjunctions. This is a solid, convergent substrate for the next session.
